question: What is the name of an elementary school in New York whose location includes Clinton Hill and neighborhoods adjacent to it, has a special education kindergarten class of average class size <= 10 each year and the fewest average violent incidents excluding 'Other Disruptive' between 2008 to 2011?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: Clinton Hill is bordered by Williamsburg to the northeast, Bedford-Stuyvesant to the east, Prospect Heights to the south/southwest, and Fort Greene to the west.
1.subquestion: What are the neighborhoods adjacent to and including Clinton Hill?

2.fact:
import io
import re
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2013-2014-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

target_ntas = {"BK69", "BK75", "BK68", "BK64", "BK72"}
target_codes = {"K003", "K009", "K011", "K046", "K056", "K067", "K270"}
filtered = df[
    df["NTA"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(target_ntas)
    & df["LOCATION_CODE"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(target_codes)
    & df["LOCATION_CATEGORY_DESCRIPTION"].astype(str).str.strip().eq("Elementary")
].copy()
nums = pd.to_numeric(filtered["LOCATION_CODE"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(\d+)$", expand=False), errors="coerce")
schools = [f"PS {int(n):03d}" for n in sorted(nums.dropna().unique())]
answer = schools + ["..."]
2.subquestion: Which elementary schools are located in <node_1 answer>?

3.fact:
import io
import re
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2008-09-class-size-school-level-detail/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

target_codes = ['K003', 'K009', 'K011', 'K046', 'K056', 'K067', 'K270']
df["SCHOOL CODE"] = df["SCHOOL CODE"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["PROGRAM TYPE"] = df["PROGRAM TYPE"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"] = pd.to_numeric(df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"], errors="coerce")
grade_col = [c for c in df.columns if c.strip() == "GRADE"][0]
filtered = df[
    df["SCHOOL CODE"].isin(target_codes)
    & df["PROGRAM TYPE"].eq("SPEC ED")
    & df[grade_col].astype(str).str.strip().str.contains("0K", na=False)
    & df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"].le(10)
].copy()
seen = set(filtered["SCHOOL CODE"].dropna().astype(str))
schools = [f"PS {int(code[1:]):03d}" for code in target_codes if code in seen]
answer = schools + ["..."]
3.subquestion: Which schools from <node_2 answer> had kindergarten class size <= 10 in 2008-09?

5.fact:
import io
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2010-2011-class-size-school-level-detail/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

target = {
    "K003": "PS 003 The Bedford Village",
    "K011": "PS 011 Purvis J. Behan",
    "K046": "PS 046 Edward C. Blum",
    "K054": "PS 054 Samuel C. Barnes",
    "K056": "PS 056 Lewis H. Latimer",
    "K256": "PS 256 Benjamin Banneker",
    "K270": "PS 270 Johann DeKalb",
}
df["SCHOOL CODE"] = df["SCHOOL CODE"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["PROGRAM TYPE"] = df["PROGRAM TYPE"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"] = pd.to_numeric(df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df[
    df["SCHOOL CODE"].isin(target)
    & df["PROGRAM TYPE"].eq("SPEC ED")
    & df["GRADE"].astype(str).str.strip().str.contains("0K", na=False)
    & df["AVERAGE CLASS SIZE"].le(10)
].copy()
seen = set(filtered["SCHOOL CODE"].dropna().astype(str))
answer = [target[code] for code in target if code in seen]
5.subquestion: Which schools from <node_2 answer> had kindergarten class size <= 10 in 2010-11?

6.fact:
import io
import re
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2008-2009-vadir-incidents/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

target = {
    "003": "PS 003 The Bedford Village",
    "009": "PS 009 Teunis G. Bergen",
    "011": "PS 011 Purvis J. Behan",
    "046": "PS 046 Edward C. Blum",
    "056": "PS 056 Lewis H. Latimer",
    "067": "PS 067 Charles A. Dorsey",
    "270": "PS 270 Johann DeKalb",
}
exclude = {"Other Disruptive", "Enrollment", "BEDS Code"}
count_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in exclude and c not in ('School Year', 'School Name', 'District Name', 'School Type')]
for c in count_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
df["_num"] = df["School Name"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(?i)\bps\s*0*(\d+)\b", expand=False).str.zfill(3)
answer = {}
for num, name in target.items():
    rows = df[df["_num"].eq(num)]
    answer[name] = int(rows[count_cols].sum(axis=1).sum()) if not rows.empty else 0
6.subquestion: How many VADIR incidents did each school in <intersection of node_3, node_4, node_5> have in 2008-09?

import io
import re
import boto3
import pandas as pd
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/2010-2011-vadir-incidents/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

target = {
    "003": "PS 003 The Bedford Village",
    "009": "PS 009 Teunis G. Bergen",
    "011": "PS 011 Purvis J. Behan",
    "046": "PS 046 Edward C. Blum",
    "056": "PS 056 Lewis H. Latimer",
    "067": "PS 067 Charles A. Dorsey",
    "270": "PS 270 Johann DeKalb",
}
exclude = {"Other Disruptive", "Enrollment", "BEDS Code"}
count_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in exclude and c not in ('School Year', 'School Name', 'District Name', 'School Type')]
for c in count_cols:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
df["_num"] = df["School Name"].astype(str).str.extract(r"(?i)\bps\s*0*(\d+)\b", expand=False).str.zfill(3)
answer = {}
for num, name in target.items():
    rows = df[df["_num"].eq(num)]
    answer[name] = int(rows[count_cols].sum(axis=1).sum()) if not rows.empty else 0
8.subquestion: How many VADIR incidents did each school in <intersection of node_3, node_4, node_5> have in 2010-11?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Get neighborhoods adjacent to Clinton Hill → {Clinton Hill, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Fort Greene, Prospect Heights, Williamsburg}
Node 2 depends on node 1: Find elementary schools in those neighborhoods → 12 schools
Nodes 3,4,5 depend on node 2: Check which schools have class size <= 10 each year
Intersection of <node_3>, <node_4>, <node_5>: Schools meeting class size criterion ALL 3 years → {PS 003, PS 009, PS 011, PS 046, PS 056, PS 067, PS 270}
Nodes 6,7,8 depend on intersection: Get VADIR counts for those 6 schools
Superlative on <node_6>, <node_7>, <node_8>: Sum VADIR, find minimum → PS 056 (9), PS 270 (13), PS 256 (22), PS 003 (25), PS 054 (31), PS 011 (61)
Final answer: PS 056 Lewis H. Latimer (fewest VADIR incidents with 9 total)

reasoning_hops.reasoning: Node 1: Get neighborhoods adjacent to Clinton Hill -> {Clinton Hill, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Fort Greene, Prospect Heights, Williamsburg}.
reasoning_hops.answer: {Clinton Hill, Bedford-Stuyvesant, Fort Greene, Prospect Heights, Williamsburg}

reasoning_hops.reasoning: Node 2 depends on node 1: find elementary schools in those neighborhoods -> 12 schools.

Nodes 3, 4, and 5 depend on node 2: check which schools have class size <= 10 in each year (2008-09, 2009-10, 2010-11). Intersection of <node_3>, <node_4>, <node_5> -> {PS 003, PS 009, PS 011, PS 046, PS 056, PS 067, PS 270}.

Nodes 6, 7, and 8 depend on the class-size intersection: collect VADIR counts (excluding Other Disruptive) for 2008-09, 2009-10, and 2010-11. Superlative on <node_6>, <node_7>, <node_8>: sum incidents and take minimum -> PS 056 Lewis H. Latimer.
